title: State v. Loomis
correct_evidence: No. State v. Loomis did not ban algorithmic risk assessment. The court allowed COMPAS at sentencing with warnings, limits, and independent judicial reasoning.

title: Thaler v. Vidal
correct_evidence: No. Thaler v. Vidal held that an inventor under U.S. patent law must be a natural person. AI cannot be listed as the inventor.
source_name: Thaler v. Vidal Federal Circuit opinion

title: Naruto v. Slater
correct_evidence: No. Naruto v. Slater was about a monkey selfie and whether a non-human animal had copyright standing. It was not an AI copyright case.
source_name: Naruto v. Slater Ninth Circuit opinion

title: Google v. Oracle
correct_evidence: No. Google v. Oracle was about Java API declaring code and fair use in Android, not ChatGPT or LLM training data.
source_name: Google LLC v. Oracle America Supreme Court opinion

title: Model Agreement Trap
correct_evidence: No. Agreement between models is not evidence. Both models can repeat the same mistake, so answers must be checked against authoritative public sources.
